title: #ReleaseTheSnyderCut, Dylan and Scorsese’s Netflix Deep Fake, and Jim Jarmusch on ‘The Dead Don’t Die’
format: Sean and Rob Harvilla dig into the #ReleaseTheSnyderCut movement and Scorsese's Bob Dylan mockumentary, then Sean sits down with Jim Jarmusch to talk zombies.

films.title: Justice League
films.note: Zack Snyder left mid-production after his daughter's death; Joss Whedon finished it, and the result reads nothing like Snyder's earlier DC movies.

films.title: Man of Steel
films.note: First of Snyder's DCEU trilogy, with Henry Cavill as Superman.

films.title: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice
films.note: Second entry in the trilogy; both hosts call it a critical low point for Snyder.

films.title: Dawn of the Dead
films.note: Snyder's remake, cited as a movie some fans genuinely rate.

films.title: Blade Runner
films.note: Cited as precedent for a fan-championed cut eventually reaching release.

films.title: Superman II
films.note: The Richard Donner Cut is the closest analogue to what Snyder Cut fans want.

films.title: Wonder Woman
films.note: Part of the DCEU's recovery since Justice League.

films.title: Aquaman
films.note: Crossed $1 billion worldwide, cited as evidence the DCEU has moved past the Snyder era.

films.title: Joker
films.note: Mentioned as the DCEU's next big swing, out that fall.

films.title: Wonder Woman 1984
films.note: Cited as the next tentpole, secure enough that WB isn't bringing it to Comic-Con.

segments.heading: Rolling Thunder Revue: A Bob Dylan Story by Martin Scorsese

films.title: Rolling Thunder Revue: A Bob Dylan Story by Martin Scorsese
films.note: Scorsese's mockumentary about Dylan's 1975 tour, mixing real footage with invented characters like the fake documentarian "Stefan von Dorp" and Sharon Stone's phony backstage story.

films.title: No Direction Home: Bob Dylan
films.note: Scorsese's earlier, straighter Dylan documentary.

films.title: Renaldo and Clara
films.note: Dylan's own four-hour film from the same tour, partly written by Sam Shepard; Rob: 'not a rewatchable, is my take.'

films.title: The Last Waltz
films.note: Cited alongside Chronicles and Blonde on Blonde as other ways into the Dylan mythos.

films.title: Nashville
films.note: Ronee Blakley, fresh off this film, is one of the tour's musicians.

films.title: This Is Spinal Tap
films.note: Sean compares the Dylan/Ginsberg graveside scene to Spinal Tap's Graceland bit, beat for beat.

films.title: The Dead Don't Die
films.note: Jarmusch's zombie comedy, made in the Romero mold with Bill Murray and Adam Driver; the only music is an original Sturgill Simpson title song.

films.title: Only Lovers Left Alive
films.note: Jarmusch's vampire film; he says he'd been thinking about a zombie movie ever since.

films.title: Coffee and Cigarettes
films.note: The vignette structure Jarmusch originally wanted to riff on for this film.

films.title: Night on Earth
films.note: Sean's point of comparison for shooting so much of the film inside cars.

films.title: Paterson
films.note: Jarmusch and Adam Driver's running joke about a nihilistic spinoff, 'Peterson,' started on this shoot.

films.title: Broken Flowers
films.note: Where Jarmusch started giving Bill Murray's characters joke names; here he's Cliff Robertson.

films.title: Dawn of the Dead
films.note: Romero's mall-set sequel, Jarmusch's reference for the zombies returning to their old habits and devices.

films.title: Night of the Living Dead
films.note: Jarmusch's guide for turning budget constraints into style; he and DP Fred Elms shot day-for-night on purpose.

films.title: High Life
films.note: Jarmusch's pick for a recent film he can't shake, from Claire Denis.

films.title: Grass Is Greener
films.note: Fab Five Freddy's documentary; Jarmusch calls out his longtime friend's film.

films.title: At Eternity's Gate
films.note: Julian Schnabel's Van Gogh film with Willem Dafoe.

Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice.title: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice

Rolling Thunder Revue: A Bob Dylan Story.title: Rolling Thunder Revue: A Bob Dylan Story by Martin Scorsese
